So the code for generating subsets can be:

void backtrack(int start, int target, vector<int>& current, vector<vector<int>>& result) {
    if (target == 0) {
        result.push_back(current);
        return;
    }
    for (int i = start; i <= target; ++i) {
        if (i > target) break;
        current.push_back(i);
        backtrack(i+1, target - i, current, result);
        current.pop_back();
    }
}

But wait, this function would generate all subsets of unique digits (each ≥ start) that sum to target. For example, for target=6, the function would generate [1,2,3], [1,5], [2,4], [6].

void backtrack(int start, int target, vector<int>& current, vector<vector<int>>& result) {
    if (target == 0) {
        result.push_back(current);
        return;
    }
    for (int i = start; i <= target; ++i) {
        if (i > target) break;
        current.push_back(i);
        backtrack(i+1, target - i, current, result);
        current.pop_back();
    }
}

But wait, this function would generate all subsets of unique digits (each ≥ start) that sum to target. For example, for target=6, the function would generate [1,2,3], [1,5], [2,4], [6].
